title: malus pumila var. dasyphylla
intro: Malus pumila var. dasyphylla, also known as the Mountain Crabapple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to China, belonging to the Malus genus. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Malus pumila var. dasyphylla include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant's bark can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
